data.title: Global chip equipment revenue tops $65B in H1 amid AI-fueled demand, SEMI says
data.description:
SEMI, the global industry association for the semiconductor and electronics manufacturing supply chain, on Friday reported a 24% year-over-year increase in global semiconductor equipment billings, reaching $33.07 billion in the second quarter.

Quarter-over-quarter, billings rose 3%, driven by strong demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM)-related DRAM applications and increased shipments to Asia.

"The global semiconductor equipment market registered a strong first half of 2025 with more than $65 billion in revenue, building on the record billings of $117 billion in 2024," said Ajit Manocha, SEMI President and CEO.

"Chipmakers continue to invest in production capacity to support advanced logic and memory innovation powering the AI wave, as well as key projects to bolster regional supply chain resilience."

Seeking Alpha analyst Julia Ostian, in a recent article, stated that the biggest debate around semiconductors right now is whether this rally is a bubble or if this sector will continue bringing impressive total returns in the future.

AI, undoubtedly, has poured gasoline on the fire, "but it’s not the only reason for the growth. Edge computing, EV adoption, industrial automation, and data center buildouts are structural forces that aren’t going away."

data.title: Mobix Labs sweetens bid to acquire Peraso with cash and stock deal
data.description:
Mobix Labs (NASDAQ:MOBX) has enhanced its bid to acquire Peraso (NASDAQ:PRSO) by adding a cash component to its original all-stock offer.

Shares of Peraso (NASDAQ:PRSO) rose nearly 25% premarket.

Mobix Labs submitted an unsolicited, non-binding proposal to acquire Peraso in June. The offer would exchange all outstanding Peraso shares for Mobix stock, valuing Peraso at about $1.20 per share—a 20% premium to its 30-day average price through June 11, 2025.

With this enhanced cash-and-stock offer, Mobix Labs has requested a meeting and has asked the Peraso board to provide a response by September 8, 2025.

Keyvan Samini, president and chief financial officer of Mobix Labs, said, “If the Peraso Board continues to refuse to engage, we are prepared to take this offer directly to Peraso stockholders. Our strong preference, however, is a cooperative, negotiated transaction that creates significant long-term value for both companies’ stockholders.”

Source: Press Release

data.title: New Found Gold to acquire Maritime Resources, creating emerging Canadian gold producer
data.description:
New Found Gold (NYSE:NFGC) +5.8% pre-market Friday after saying it agreed to acquire Maritime Resources (OTCPK:MRTMF), which owns the Hammerdown gold project in Newfoundland that is expected to ramp up to full production in early 2026, for an implied equity value of C$292M.

New Found Gold's (NYSE:NFGC) Queensway gold project, which delivered a positive preliminary economic assessment earlier this year and is targeting phase 1 production in 2027, also is located in Newfoundland, and the combined company is expected to create significant operational synergies through available infrastructure and anticipated cash flow from Hammerdown once in full production to support Queensway's development.

Under the deal terms, each Maritime (OTCPK:MRTMF) shareholder will receive 0.75 of a New Found Gold common share (NFGC) in exchange for each Maritime share; at closing, existing New Found Gold and Maritime shareholders will own ~69% and 31%, respectively, of the pro forma company on a fully diluted basis.

data.title: Supremex appoints Normand Macaulay as CFO and Corporate Secretary
Orsted cuts FY EBITDA guidance owing to lower than normal wind speed
data.description:
Orsted (OTCPK:DNNGY) cut its full-year 2025 earnings guidance due to lower-than-normal offshore wind speeds experienced in July and August.

The company estimates that this reduction in wind speeds will lower EBITDA by approximately DKK1.2 billion (about $187 million).

Additionally, a construction delay at its Greater Changhua 2b offshore wind farm project in Taiwan is expected to further reduce earnings by DKK300 million.

The Danish company sees FY EBITDA to be in the range of DKK24 billion to KRW27 billion vs. previous figure of DKK25 billion to DKK28 billion. However, the company said that its medium-term targets remain unchanged.

Gross investment guidance for 2025 remains unchanged between DKK50 billion and DKK54 billion.

data.title: Tesla makes the case why shareholders should vote for an investment in xAI
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) will hold a shareholder meeting on November 6. One of the votes will be on the proposal regarding board authorization of an investment in xAI

In its SEC filing, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) highlighted that it is transforming into a leading artificial intelligence, robotics, and energy company, with its mission to advance technology for human benefit and advance sustainable energy.

xAI, a company focused on building AI to accelerate human scientific discovery, was noted to have developed Grok, an AI assistant already integrated into Tesla vehicles to enhance user experience, vehicle functionality, and autonomous driving capabilities.

Shareholders were advised that the synergies between Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and xAI are evident, as both companies share a commitment to advancing technology for human benefit, with xAI’s AI expertise complementing Tesla’s (TSLA) advancements in autonomous driving, robotics, and energy optimization.

"Tesla’s strategic investments in AI are critical to maintaining its competitive edge in the rapidly evolving AI and robotics industries, and a direct investment in xAI would strengthen Tesla’s access to cutting-edge AI technologies, talent, and intellectual property... xAI’s mission aligns with Tesla’s, creating opportunities for collaborative innovation in areas such as vehicle AI, energy grid optimization, and humanoid robotics, which could enhance Tesla’s product offerings and market leadership"

In summary, the Texas-based company said an investment in xAI would provide Tesla (TSLA) with a stake in a major AI player, potentially yielding significant financial returns while fostering technological advancements that benefit customers and shareholders.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) rose 2.0% in premarket trading.

data.description:

data.title: Sono Group uplists to Nasdaq capital market under ticker "SSM"
Tesla rises after board proposes new pay plan for Musk
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) has asked shareholders to approve a new performance-based pay package for CEO Elon Musk, according to a Friday filing.

The proposal includes 12 tranches of stock awards to be granted if the EV maker hits certain milestones over the next decade. It would also grant Musk greater voting control over the company and aspiring robotics titan, a demand he has voiced since early 2024.

To unlock the first tranche, Tesla must nearly double its valuation to $2 trillion and hit a cumulative 20 million vehicle production and deliveries milestone.

Additional goals for Musk to reach under the 2025 CEO Performance Award include hitting adjusted EBITDA benchmarks and and rolling out new or expanded product offerings (including 1 million Robotaxis in commercial operation and delivery of 1 million AI Bots).

"In 2018, Elon had to grow Tesla by billions; in 2025, he has to grow Tesla by trillions — to be exact, he must create nearly $7.5 trillion in value for shareholders for him to receive the full award," the filing read.

Chairwoman Robyn Denholm told CNBC that the plan is designed to keep Musk “motivated and focused on delivering for the company.” Musk remains politically entangled while juggling multiple ventures, including SpaceX, The Boring Company, Neuralink, and the AI startup xAI, which has merged with his social platform X.

In addition, the automaker is also seeking shareholder approval for a potential investment in xAI.

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares rose 2% premarket Friday.

data.title: Smith & Wesson tops estimates for a seasonal slow period for firearm demand
data.description:
Smith & Wesson Brands (NASDAQ:SWBI) topped expectations with its FQ1 earnings report. 

Net sales for the quarter were $85.1 million, down 3.7% year-over-year, but still well above analyst expectations. Gross margin fell from a year ago to 26.0% of sales, as pressures from production costs and competitive pricing factored in. The company's non-GAAP EPS loss of -$0.08 was better than the -$0.12 loss anticipated by analysts. 

Despite the top-line and profitability challenges, Smith & Wesson Brands (NASDAQ:SWBI) management emphasized positive signals, pointing to robust demand for new products and significant market share gains in the handgun segment, with shipments up over 35% in the sporting goods channel. Notably, new firearms launches accounted for 37.3% of total sales. Channel inventories at distributors declined by more than 13% versus a year ago, indicating healthy retail sell-through, though Smith & Wesson’s own inventory increased as part of normal seasonal patterns.

"Our performance during the seasonal slow period for firearms demonstrates the strength of our brand and the ongoing success of our innovation strategy. Innovation remains a cornerstone of our strategy, with new products accounting for 37.3% of sales in the first quarter," highlighted CEO Mark Smith. "With a strong pipeline of new products upcoming, we will continue to invest in innovation to keep the line fresh, and ensure we maintain our leadership position," he added.

Looking ahead, the company expects FQ2 sales to land 3% to 5% below last year’s comparable period. On the earnings conference call, Smith & Wesson Brands (SWBI) warned that the FQ2 gross margin rate would be similar to the FQ1 mark. Shares of Smith & Wesson Brands (SWBI) edged 0.5% higher in premarket action on Friday. The firearms stock is down 19% on a year-to-date basis.
